title: Koenigia yatagaiana
taxonomy: Koenigia yatagaiana is classified in the family Polygonaceae, commonly known as the buckwheat or knotweed family. This family consists of a large number of flowering plant genera, including buckwheat, rhubarb, and wild buckwheat. Koenigia is a genus of flowering plants, and Koenigia yatagaiana is a specific species within this genus. It is native to the Himalayas and surrounding regions. Koenigia yatagaiana is often found growing in mountainous areas at high elevations.
uses: Koenigia yatagaiana is a plant species native to high-altitude regions in the Himalayas. Koenigia yatagaiana has been traditionally used in the local folk medicine for its potential anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties.
